kept.text:
amazon $AMZN is by far the most underrated ai company in the world right now and nobody sees it:

> owns 20% of anthropic. owns major stake in openai (investing $35B)

> primary provider of claude, chatgpt and any top ai model via amazon bedrock

> EVERY government organisation uses amazon to manage and serve ai models

> AWS revenue due to ai is through the fucking roof.

> amazon trainium ai chips are sold-out including their new chip that hasn’t released yet. demand is at ath 

> anthropic committed to using 1-2M amazon chips to train claude 

> amazon robotics division is one of the largest in the world. data can be used to train top robotics models

> spending the most on ai capex ($200B) this year alone. ceo Jassy says demand has already justified the spend. if they’re right they’ll be right in a huge way.

my bet is tmrw they announce a major partnership with openai that includes ability to distribute multiple enterprise versions of chatgpt to amazons fortune 500 clients.

amazon has silently become the #1 distributor of the worlds best models

 that alone is worth trillions.

kept.text:
TRANSFER LEARNING book [390 pages]: https://t.co/L6SrJ4PXuY

Amazon Summary:
"Transfer learning deals with how systems can quickly adapt themselves to new situations, tasks and environments. It gives machine learning systems the ability to leverage auxiliary data and models to help solve target problems when there is only a small amount of data available. This makes such systems more reliable and robust, keeping the machine learning model faced with unforeseeable changes from deviating too much from expected performance. At an enterprise level, transfer learning allows knowledge to be reused so experience gained once can be repeatedly applied to the real world."
